data.task:
ATOM 1 of the Play System priority — DECISION-BEFORE-CODE. Amend ADR-0010 (cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md) to define the Play taxonomy that all later schema and dispatch code will derive from. This is a living-ADR amendment per ADR-0014 — ADD a dated, attributed amendment; do NOT rewrite or delete the already-accepted Decision/Consequences sections (Vocabulary, create-priority, Authoring lifecycle, Homes).

WRITE SCOPE (only this file): cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md

WHAT TO WRITE — an amendment section (header like '> **2026-06-19 amendment (founder, run_151) — Play taxonomy: trigger class × execution model.**') that defines the five Play classes the priority names:
  1. prompt-only Play
  2. hybrid Play (deterministic precheck/gate)
  3. lifecycle-triggered Play
  4. persona-requested Play
  5. tool/API-triggered Play

CRITICAL DESIGN POINT — these five are NOT one flat mutually-exclusive list. They span TWO orthogonal axes, and the amendment must say so explicitly because atom 2's schema (already drafted in the priority) carries 'trigger class' AND 'deterministic step' as SEPARATE optional fields:
  - AXIS A — execution model: prompt-only (markdown injected to the LLM, no code spine) vs hybrid (an optional deterministic step whose captured result gates or feeds the LLM layer). The founder's framing: 'a Play may be LLM-driven but also have deterministic code behind it.'
  - AXIS B — trigger/caller class: lifecycle-triggered (runner/daemon invokes at a lifecycle point, no persona discretion), persona-requested (a persona asks via a typed handoff; runner validates before dispatch), tool/API-triggered (invoked by a tool/API call).
  Make clear a single Play has one trigger class AND one execution model (e.g. wrap-up = lifecycle-triggered + prompt-only; code-review = persona-requested + potentially hybrid).

BACKWARD-COMPAT (load-bearing): the existing Play axis 'kind: headless | interactive' (write-authority/interactivity) must be RECONCILED, not erased. State plainly that the existing headless/interactive attribute is a THIRD, independent axis (write authority) that survives unchanged, and that the new trigger-class and execution-model axes are additive. Atom 2 will keep all existing prompt-only Plays valid, so the ADR must not declare anything that forces a breaking schema change.

For EACH class, give the defining attributes atom 2's schema will encode: who/what may invoke it (allowed callers), how it is triggered (trigger class), whether it runs deterministic code (execution model), and how its output is validated/committed. Keep it to the decision itself — do NOT design the TypeScript schema, the manifest, or dispatch here (those are atoms 2–7).

RESPECT THE PRIORITY BOUNDARY: do not reopen one-level dispatch; do not introduce PlayAssignment multi-binding; do not propose full Play-body injection into every prompt.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (judgment-gated, one-shot — NOT loop-amenable):
  - All five named classes defined, each with allowed-caller / trigger / execution-model / validation-commit attributes.
  - The two-axis (trigger × execution) structure is explicit, and headless/interactive is explicitly reconciled as a surviving independent axis.
  - Amendment is dated 2026-06-19, attributed to founder/run_151, in the living-ADR style already used in this file; existing accepted sections untouched.
  - Only cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md changed.

MUST NOT BREAK: the existing accepted ADR-0010 decisions and their wording; the ADR-0020 onboarding-Playbook exception note; any cross-ADR references. This amendment is the source the founder accepts before any schema change (atom 2).

data.reason: ADR-0010 amendment defines all five Play classes across three orthogonal axes (trigger × execution × surviving headless/interactive), additive and backward-compatible, living-ADR style, existing sections untouched, scope respected; docs-only so no tests apply.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 3 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): cocoder/decisions/0010-taxonomy-and-authoring.md.
